import edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast.synopses.PointSampler

val sampleSize = 1000
val sampleRatio = 0.5

// Call the static method on PointSampler with the pre-loaded featuresRDD
val sample: Array[Array[Double]] = PointSampler.pointSample(featuresRDD, sampleSize, sampleRatio)

// Verify the dimension-major structure
assert(sample != null, "Sample array should not be null")
assert(sample.length >= 2, s"Expected at least 2 dimensions (X and Y), but got ${sample.length}")

val numPointsX = sample(0).length
val numPointsY = sample(1).length

// Assert correctness of the returned structure and constraints
assert(numPointsX == numPointsY, s"Mismatch in dimension lengths: X has $numPointsX, Y has $numPointsY")
assert(numPointsX <= sampleSize, s"Number of sampled points ($numPointsX) exceeded the sampleSize limit ($sampleSize)")

println(s"__CHECK__ pointSample $numPointsX")

import java.util

reprojectGeometry.code:
import org.geotools.referencing.CRS
import edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast.cg.Reprojector

val originalGeom = featuresRDD.first().getGeometry
val srid = if (originalGeom.getSRID == 0) 4326 else originalGeom.getSRID
val sourceCRS = CRS.decode(s"EPSG:$srid", true)
val targetCRS = CRS.decode("EPSG:3857", true)

val projectedGeom = Reprojector.reprojectGeometry(originalGeom, sourceCRS, targetCRS)

assert(projectedGeom != null, "Projected geometry should not be null")
assert(projectedGeom ne originalGeom, "Projected geometry should be a new instance")

if (!originalGeom.isEmpty && srid != 3857) {
  val origCoord = originalGeom.getCoordinate
  val projCoord = projectedGeom.getCoordinate
  if (origCoord.x != 0.0 && origCoord.y != 0.0) {
    assert(origCoord.x != projCoord.x || origCoord.y != projCoord.y, "Coordinates should change after reprojection")
  }
}

println(s"__CHECK__ reprojectGeometry ${projectedGeom.getGeometryType}")

import edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast.io.SpatialWriter
import edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast.common.BeastOptions

// Coalesce to 1 partition to avoid distributed-write merge errors when saving
val coalescedFeatures = featuresRDD.coalesce(1)
val originalCount = coalescedFeatures.count()
val outPath = output_dir + "/test_out.shp"

// Save the features to disk as a Shapefile
SpatialWriter.saveFeatures(coalescedFeatures, "shapefile", outPath, new BeastOptions())

// Read the saved features back to verify
val readBackRDD = sc.shapefile(outPath)
val readBackCount = readBackRDD.count()

// Assert that the number of features written matches the number read back
assert(readBackCount == originalCount, s"Expected $originalCount features, but read back $readBackCount")
println(s"__CHECK__ saveFeatures $readBackCount")

import edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast._
import edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast.indexing.{RSGrovePartitioner, IndexHelper}
import edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast.common.BeastOptions
import java.io.File

// 1. Spatially partition the RDD (Crucial Precondition)
val partitioned = featuresRDD.spatialPartition(classOf[RSGrovePartitioner])

// 2. Define a fresh output path to avoid "directory already exists" errors
val outPath = new File(output_dir, "index2_out").getPath

// 3. Save the partitioned RDD with a master index file
val opts = new BeastOptions().set("oformat", "geojson")
IndexHelper.saveIndex2(partitioned, outPath, opts)

// 4. Read the saved index back to verify correctness
val loaded = sc.spatialFile(outPath)
val originalCount = featuresRDD.count()
val loadedCount = loaded.count()

assert(loadedCount == originalCount, s"Expected $originalCount features, but got $loadedCount")
println(s"__CHECK__ saveIndex2 $loadedCount")

val r2 = sc.parallelize(sampleFeatures)

// Since r1 and r2 contain the exact same features, each feature must at least intersect with its own MBR.
assert(count >= n, s"Expected at least $n intersections (self-intersections), got $count")
println(s"__CHECK__ spatialJoinBNLJ $count")

import edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast.cg.SpatialJoinAlgorithms.ESJPredicate
import edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast.operations.SpatialJoin

val originalCount = featuresRDD.count()

// Precondition: r1 MUST be spatially partitioned using a disjoint partitioner
val partitionedR1 = featuresRDD.spatialPartition(classOf[RSGrovePartitioner])

// Execute Repartition Join (REPJ) with partitioned r1 and unpartitioned r2
val joined = SpatialJoin.spatialJoinRepJ(
  partitionedR1, 
  featuresRDD, 
  ESJPredicate.Intersects
)

val joinedCount = joined.count()

// Correctness check: A self-join with the Intersects predicate must yield 
// at least as many pairs as there are features in the original dataset.
assert(joinedCount >= originalCount, s"Self-join should produce at least $originalCount matches, but got $joinedCount")

println(s"__CHECK__ spatialJoinRepJ $joinedCount")

splitGeometryAcrossDateLine.code:
import org.locationtech.jts.geom.{Coordinate, GeometryFactory}
import edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast.cg.GeometryQuadSplitter

val gf = new GeometryFactory()

// Create a polygon that crosses the dateline (width > 180 degrees)
val coordsCrossing = Array(
  new Coordinate(170.0, 10.0),
  new Coordinate(-170.0, 10.0),
  new Coordinate(-170.0, -10.0),
  new Coordinate(170.0, -10.0),
  new Coordinate(170.0, 10.0)
)
val polygonCrossing = gf.createPolygon(coordsCrossing)
// SRID must be set to 4326 to satisfy the method's requirement
polygonCrossing.setSRID(4326)

// Call the static method to split the geometry
val splitGeom = GeometryQuadSplitter.splitGeometryAcrossDateLine(polygonCrossing)
val numGeometries = splitGeom.getNumGeometries

// Verify that the polygon was split into two parts
assert(numGeometries == 2, s"Expected 2 geometries after splitting a dateline-crossing polygon, but got $numGeometries")

// Create a normal polygon that does not cross the dateline
val coordsNormal = Array(
  new Coordinate(10.0, 10.0),
  new Coordinate(20.0, 10.0),
  new Coordinate(20.0, -10.0),
  new Coordinate(10.0, -10.0),
  new Coordinate(10.0, 10.0)
)
val polygonNormal = gf.createPolygon(coordsNormal)
polygonNormal.setSRID(4326)

val splitGeomNormal = GeometryQuadSplitter.splitGeometryAcrossDateLine(polygonNormal)
assert(splitGeomNormal.getNumGeometries == 1, s"Expected 1 geometry for a non-crossing polygon, but got ${splitGeomNormal.getNumGeometries}")

println(s"__CHECK__ splitGeometryAcrossDateLine $numGeometries")

import org.apache.hadoop.fs.Path
import edu.ucr.cs.bdlab.beast.common.BeastOptions

val path = new Path(raster_tif)
val fileSystem = path.getFileSystem(sc.hadoopConfiguration)
val reader = new GeoTiffReader()
reader.initialize(fileSystem, raster_tif, "0", new BeastOptions())

val ids: Iterator[Int] = reader.metadata.tileIDs
val idsList = ids.toList
val numTiles = idsList.size

// The number of tile IDs in the metadata should match the number of tiles loaded in the high-level RasterRDD
val expectedNumTiles = rasterRDD.count().toInt

assert(numTiles == expectedNumTiles, s"Expected $expectedNumTiles tile IDs, but got $numTiles")
assert(numTiles > 0, "Tile IDs iterator should not be empty")
assert(idsList.distinct.size == numTiles, "Tile IDs should be unique")

println(s"__CHECK__ tileIDs $numTiles")
